center.summary: The Malay Peninsula is located in Mainland Southeast Asia.

birds.evidence: K.|last2=McGowan|first3=Guy M.|last3=Kirwan|first4=Nigel|last4=Collar|first5=David|last5=Christie|url-access=subscription}} The Malayan crestless fireback is found in the Malay Peninsula and Sumatra

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |year=2024 |title=Pellorneum rostratum |volume=2024 |article-number=e.T22715792A260307448 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2024-2.RLTS.T22715792A260307448.en |access-date=27 May 2025}} | genus = Pellorneum | species = rostratum | authority = (Blyth, 1842) | synonyms = }} The Malayan swamp babbler (Pellorneum rostratum), previously named the white-chested babbler, is a species of bird in the ground babbler family, Pellorneidae, that is found in the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, the Riau Islands, the Lingga Islands and the island of Belitung

birds.evidence: It is found in Sumatra and the Malay Peninsula

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This species is a native resident of lowland forests across southern Thailand, southern Myanmar, the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, Riau Archipelago, and the islands of Banjak, Bangka, Belitung, Borneo, and Natuna. It hangs around the middle storeys of lowland semi-evergreen and evergreen forests, which include freshwater swamp forests, in lowlands and in hills. It is sometimes in the lower storeys and borders of forests, and in mature secondary growth forests but tends not to live in mangroves, in cultivated areas, or in open country. The presence of lianas is also favored by this species. The highest sightings of individuals vary depending on the location: in the Malay Peninsula, the highest sighting was at , and on Mount Kinabalu, it was at , while it was only about in Thailand. In Kalimantan, the height the birds were inhabiting varied between

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: During winter some birds migrate south to the Malay Peninsula and Sumatra. It is most commonly found in deciduous and evergreen primary and secondary forests,{{cite book | last1=Fry | first1=C.H

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The Chinese blue flycatcher is native to mid-southern China; it winters to the Tenasserim Hills and the Malay Peninsula. It previously was considered a subspecies of the blue-throated blue flycatcher

birds.evidence: This cuckooshrike is found in the Thai-Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, Borneo, the Philippines, and many small islands in the area, such as the Kangean Islands and Natuna Islands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|Fire-tufted barbet at Fraser's Hill, Malaysia, August 1994 The fire-tufted barbet inhabits broad-leaved evergreen montane forests between on the Malay Peninsula and Sumatra. Three fire-tufted barbets observed on Mount Gede in West Java between 2003 and 2005 are thought to have escaped from aviaries in this area.{{Cite journal |author1=Van Balen, B

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This species is found in Tenasserim, the Thai-Malay Peninsula, Sumatra and its satellite islands, Borneo and Palawan. It is locally extinct in Singapore. It lives in the canopy of lowland broadleaf forest, forest edges, peat swamp forest, well-grown secondary forest, and coastal and mangrove forest. It is found up to in elevation, mainly below . It sometimes visits wooded gardens near forests

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: 16-18). They also may also be found in East Asia across the southern part of the Russian Far East, eastern China, and occasionally in Southeast Asia from Myanmar and Thailand down through the Malay Peninsula.Spierenburg, P

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It migrates through eastern China to winter in the Malay Peninsula, Borneo, the Philippines, Java and Sumatra; a few birds remain around hot springs in Japan

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It breeds in South China and winters in the Malay Peninsula

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in submountain forests from the eastern Himalayas and northern Myanmar to southern China, western and southern Thailand, the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra and Borneo

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: thumb|Belait District, Brunei, Borneo, female and male Wrinkled hornbill The wrinkled hornbill or Sunda wrinkled hornbill (Rhabdotorrhinus corrugatus) is a medium-large hornbill which is found in forest in the Thai-Malay Peninsula, Sumatra and Borneo

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is found in the Malay Peninsula, Anambas Islands, Sumatra, Banyak Islands, Batu Islands, Riau Islands, Lingga Islands and the Natuna Islands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The chestnut-winged babbler occurs in the Malay Peninsula from southern Thailand to Singapore, and in Sumatra

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: Found in far north-eastern India, eastern Bangladesh, Burma, Thailand, Malay Peninsula, Borneo and Indonesia

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It occurs in the Eastern Himalayan foothills, Myanmar, Thailand, northern Indochina and south to the Malay Peninsula and the islands of Sumatra and Borneo

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The green broadbill is distributed in broadleaved evergreen forests of Borneo, Sumatra, and the Malay Peninsula where it frequents lowland and lower montane rainforest

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The red-bearded bee-eater (Nyctyornis amictus) is a large species of bee-eater found in southern Myanmar, the Thai-Malay Peninsula, Borneo, Sumatra and nearby smaller islands

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: The golden-whiskered barbet ranges from the Malay Peninsula to Sumatra and Borneo, where it inhabits tropical and subtropical moist broadleaf forests and montane forest. It has also been sighted in cacao plantation and second growth forests but is rare in evergreen and swamp forests

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |date=2017-02-01 |title=Spatial analysis of habitat conservation for hornbills: A case study of royal belum-temengor forest complex in Perak State Park, Malaysia |url=https://www.semanticscholar.org/paper/Spatial-analysis-of-habitat-conservation-for-A-case-Misni-Rasam/a629ad29e0f3d8c376379b793b4845b3a0755eb1?p2df |journal=Pertanika journal of social science and humanities}} It is found in lowland and montane, tropical and subtropical climates and in mountain rain forests up to 1,400 metres in Borneo, Sumatra, Java, the Malay Peninsula, Singapore, and southern Thailand

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It can be found in the Malay Peninsula, Singapore, Sumatra and Borneo. It is a bird of open woodland, although island forms prefer a higher tree density

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This partridge is found in certain areas of the Malay Peninsula. It is known to reside in the countries of Malaysia and Thailand, and vagrancy has not been reported. Migratory behaviors have not been reported, and this bird is presumed sedentary
